title: Common denominators before adding fractions
summary: A focused practice resource for upper primary learners who are preparing to add or compare fractions. It helps students rewrite fractions with a shared denominator before moving to addition problems.
subject: Mathematics
originalTextPreview: Best for students who can name the numerator and denominator but still need a reliable process for making fraction parts the same size.

title: Add fractions with common denominators
summary: A grade 4-style practice set where denominators already match. Students focus on adding numerators, keeping the denominator unchanged, and checking whether the answer form is reasonable.
subject: Mathematics
originalTextPreview: Useful after students understand what the denominator represents and need repeated question practice.

title: Add and subtract fractions with unlike denominators
summary: A grade 5 unit pathway that starts from visual strategies and moves toward common denominators, unlike-denominator addition, subtraction, mixed numbers, and word problems.
subject: Mathematics
originalTextPreview: Good for students who can solve like-denominator questions but lose accuracy once denominators differ.

title: Multiply fractions and whole numbers
summary: A fourth-grade unit for building fraction multiplication with models, number lines, unit fractions, mixed numbers, and word problems.
subject: Mathematics
originalTextPreview: Helpful for learners moving from repeated addition to multiplying a whole number by a fraction.

title: Multiply fractions and whole numbers word problems
summary: A practice resource for applying fraction multiplication to story problems. It supports students who know the computation but need help choosing the operation.
subject: Mathematics
originalTextPreview: Good for checking whether students can translate a story into a fraction multiplication expression.

title: Write fractions as decimals with tenths and hundredths
summary: A practice set for converting fractions with denominators of 10 or 100 into decimal form, supporting place value and decimal notation.
subject: Mathematics
originalTextPreview: Best for students learning that denominator 10 and 100 connect directly to decimal place value.

title: Add decimals with tenths and hundredths
summary: A fifth-grade decimal unit that links common fractions and decimals before moving into estimating and adding decimals.
subject: Mathematics
originalTextPreview: Useful when students line up digits incorrectly or forget the meaning of tenths and hundredths.

title: Volume with unit cubes and rectangular prisms
summary: A fifth-grade unit about measuring volume using unit cubes, rectangular prisms, decomposition, and volume word problems.
subject: Mathematics
originalTextPreview: Good for learners who understand area but need a concrete bridge to three-dimensional measurement.

title: Compare fractions using decimals or common denominators
summary: A concept guide showing two student-friendly ways to compare fractions: converting to decimals or rewriting with the same denominator.
subject: Mathematics
originalTextPreview: Helpful when students repeatedly choose the fraction with the larger denominator as the larger value.

title: Connect decimals, fractions, and percentages
summary: A visual reference connecting common fractions such as one half and one quarter with decimal and percentage forms.
subject: Mathematics
originalTextPreview: Useful for learners who can compute fractions but do not yet see that 1/2, 0.5, and 50% are the same amount.

title: Perimeter as the distance around a shape
summary: A geometry guide introducing perimeter as the distance around a two-dimensional shape, with examples and formulas for common shapes.
subject: Mathematics
originalTextPreview: Good for students who confuse perimeter with area or forget to include all sides of a shape.

title: Rectangle area and perimeter in one place
summary: A geometry reference for rectangles that explains right angles, opposite sides, area, and perimeter, helping students choose the right operation.
subject: Mathematics
originalTextPreview: Useful for word problems where students must decide whether a question asks for covering space or distance around an edge.

title: Square geometry: equal sides, area, and perimeter
summary: A student-friendly explanation of square properties, area, perimeter, and diagonals for early geometry review.
subject: Mathematics
originalTextPreview: Good for reviewing why all sides of a square are equal and how that changes perimeter calculations.

title: Rhombus properties, area, and perimeter
summary: A geometry guide explaining rhombus properties and perimeter, useful as an extension after squares and rectangles.
subject: Mathematics
originalTextPreview: Useful for students who think every four-sided shape with equal sides must be a square.

title: Parallelogram area and properties
summary: A geometry guide for understanding opposite sides, opposite angles, and the base-times-height area rule for parallelograms.
subject: Mathematics
originalTextPreview: Good for students who multiply side lengths without identifying the perpendicular height.

title: Upper primary fraction activities for reasoning
summary: A collection of fraction activities for ages 7 to 11 designed to deepen conceptual understanding through matching, visualising, folding, and explaining fractions.
subject: Mathematics
originalTextPreview: Good for students who can follow a procedure but need richer tasks to explain and represent fraction ideas.

title: Primary problems for fractions, decimals, percentages, and ratio
summary: A primary maths collection grouping problem-solving tasks around fractions, decimals, percentages, ratio, and proportion.
subject: Mathematics
originalTextPreview: Best for students who need non-routine questions that require noticing patterns and explaining reasoning.

title: Fractions Unpacked progression through problem solving
summary: A progression of curriculum-linked fraction tasks that support conceptual development through problem solving rather than repeated procedure only.
subject: Mathematics
originalTextPreview: Useful for choosing follow-up tasks after a student shows a specific fraction misconception.

title: Fraction wall activities for equivalent fractions
summary: A primary maths collection including fraction wall tasks where learners compare and match equivalent fractions visually.
subject: Mathematics
originalTextPreview: Good for students who need to see why two differently written fractions can name the same amount.

title: Fractions Jigsaw for equivalent fraction matching
summary: A puzzle-style task where pieces connect when the touching edges contain equivalent fractions, encouraging careful checking and reasoning.
subject: Mathematics
originalTextPreview: Useful as an engaging extension after equivalent fraction practice.

title: Primary fractions review and practice
summary: A BBC Bitesize fractions topic page for revising fraction ideas and classroom-style examples suitable for primary learners.
subject: Mathematics
originalTextPreview: Helpful as a broad revision link when a student needs to revisit the whole topic rather than one subskill.

title: Past simple sentences for young English learners
summary: A child-friendly grammar resource with explanations, games, and printable practice for past simple sentences.
subject: English
originalTextPreview: Helpful for learners who know the event order but mix present and past tense when writing.

title: Past simple questions for young English learners
summary: A British Council Kids grammar practice resource for asking and answering questions about the past using did and time words.
subject: English
originalTextPreview: Useful when learners write past tense statements correctly but struggle to form questions.

title: Past simple stories, chants, and grammar games
summary: A collection of LearnEnglish Kids resources tagged for past simple, including stories, chants, videos, and writing prompts.
subject: English
originalTextPreview: Good for younger learners who benefit from hearing grammar in stories rather than only seeing rules.

title: English learning resources for parents and children
summary: Cambridge English resources for children and families, useful for finding language practice activities suitable for young learners.
subject: English
originalTextPreview: Useful as a general support page for families looking for age-appropriate English practice.

title: Light and shadows for primary science
summary: A short science support video for understanding how light travels and how shadows form, useful beside hands-on classroom observation.
subject: Science
originalTextPreview: Useful for students who need to connect classroom vocabulary with real observations of light sources and blocked light.

title: Decimal place value foundation
summary: A Khan Academy unit for understanding place value in decimal numbers before moving into operations with decimals.
subject: Mathematics
originalTextPreview: Best before decimal addition and subtraction when students are still unsure what each decimal digit means.

title: Multi-digit multiplication and division review
summary: A fifth-grade unit for reviewing multi-digit multiplication, long division, and the arithmetic fluency needed for fraction and measurement problems.
subject: Mathematics
originalTextPreview: Useful when students make arithmetic slips inside otherwise correct word-problem strategies.

title: Coordinate plane basics for grade 5
summary: A coordinate plane unit that supports graph reading, ordered pairs, and early algebraic thinking for upper primary students.
subject: Mathematics
originalTextPreview: Good for students preparing to read points and patterns on a grid.

title: Mean, median, and mode for simple data sets
summary: A Math is Fun guide to basic averages and data summaries, useful for primary statistics and chart interpretation practice.
subject: Mathematics
originalTextPreview: Useful for learners who can add numbers but need to understand what an average represents.
